title: gomphocarpus undulatus
intro: Gomphocarpus undulatus, also known as the strangled bitters or devil's cotton, is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Gomphocarpus undulatus include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
